CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFENDANT SOLANA FOUNDATION, A SWISS FOUNDATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (PART 2 OF 2 FOR PURPOSED OF ENTRY OF TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Text Ruling

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SOLANA FOUNDATION'S PARTIAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Gregory N. Wolfe of Nagy Wolfe Appleton LLP (646.494.4900, greg@nagylaw.com) appearing via Zoom for defendant and moving party;
Patrick Hammon and Andrew Parkhurst of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (650.233.4500, patrick.hammon@pillsburylaw.com, andrew.parkhurst@pillsburylaw.com) for plaintiffs and responding parties.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Solana Foundation's demurrer to all seven causes of action alleged by plaintiff Inflect, Inc. in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for quantum meruit and overruled as to all other causes of action. Solana's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Solana's late-filed reply memorandum was considered by the court.

As to the first and second causes of action for breach of written contract and breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, Inflect, Inc. sufficiently alleged that it is a third-party beneficiary of the Server Inventory Services Reduction Agreement (Agreement). Determination of whether a person or entity is a third-party beneficiary is fact-intensive based on all the relevant circumstances of the making of the contract. Nothing in the Agreement rules out Inflect, Inc. as a third-party beneficiary. The fact that Inflect, Inc. was not to receive tokens does not preclude it from benefiting from the Agreement by receiving compensation other than in tokens and there is language in the Agreement supporting that Inflect, Inc.'s allegation that the named contracting parties intended that Inflect, Inc. would do so.

As to the third cause of action for quantum meruit, the alleged basis for recovery (Inflect, Inc.'s performance of a cost-reduction services for Solana) is the same as the basis for recovery under the Agreement and thus runs afoul of the rule that quantum meruit recovery is not available where the same subject matter is addressed in an express contract.

As to the fourth and fifth causes of action for intentional and negligent interference with prospective economic relations, Inflect, Inc. sufficiently alleged that Solana committed independently wrongful (tortious) conduct consisting of, among other things, actionable defamation and disparagement. Solana's arguments that all of the independent wrongful conduct allegedly committed by it are conclusions and/or non-actionable are belied by the language of the first amended complaint.

As to the sixth and seventh causes of action for intentional interference with contractual relations and inducing breach of contract, Inflect, Inc. sufficiently alleged actionable interference by Solana with Inflect, Inc.'s contract with Service Provider 2. Contrary to Solana's reading of them, the judicially noticeable documents do not show that Inflect, Inc. committed an antecedent breach of its contract with Service Provider 2.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Aug-06-2025 Continued To Aug-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SOLANA FOUNDATION's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Continued to August 20, 2025, on the court's own motion. A motion to quash service of summons is an unusual motion in that it requires no factual showing. Rather, when it is made, it shifts the burden to the plaintiff to show evidence that service was proper. Any evidence rebutting the plaintiff's factual showing is usually presented in reply. Here, significant new argument and evidence was adduced on reply. The court grants leave to plaintiffs Inflect, Inc. to file a surreply of not more than 10 pages of briefing by August 13, 2025. Inflect must seek leave of the court (by ex parte is fine with proper notice to the specially appearing defendant) to file any surreply evidence, which in turn will trigger a surreply opportunity for the specially appearing defendant.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
